channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TMA issues safety warning for Lake Nyasa fishers as rains intensify
item.description: The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has issued a serious warning for fishers along Lake Nyasa due to forecasted thunderstorms and strong winds that could be dangerous for small boats and tradi...

item.title: Saskatchewan industries face labour challenges as thousands of foreign worker permits expire
item.description: Industries across Saskatchewan are preparing for significant labour shortages as a large number of temporary foreign worker permits are set to expire this year. The Canadian Federation of Independent ...

item.title: Alex Johnston Breaks Australian Try-Scoring Record with 213 NRL Tries
item.description: Alex Johnston has cemented his place in rugby league history by surpassing Ken Irvine’s long-standing Australian record of 212 tries, reaching 213 after scoring two tries against the Roosters. While J...

item.title: Minor 4.0 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Near Karachi, Tremors Reported in Landhi, Khurramabad, Malir and Korangi
item.description: On Friday, 14 March 2026, a 4-magnitude earthquake struck with its epicentre located around 100 kilometres south of Karachi, at a shallow depth of 10 kilometres. Residents across the city felt the jol...

item.title: Independent ethics adviser finds no breach in Mandelson ambassador appointment
item.description: The Prime Minister's independent ethics adviser, Sir Laurie Magnus, has declined to investigate Sir Keir Starmer regarding his appointment of Lord Mandelson as ambassador to the United States. The dec...

item.title: OPM Singer Maki Opens Up About Overcoming Past Rejections in the Music Industry
item.description: Filipino singer-songwriter Maki, known for his hit songs in the OPM scene, recently shared in an exclusive interview with PUSH how he dealt with numerous rejections early in his career. He admitted th...

item.title: President Ruto Highlights Affordable Housing's Role in Job Creation and Economic Development in Nandi
item.description: President William Ruto visited Nandi County where he strongly defended his government's Affordable Housing Programme, calling it a vital pillar for creating jobs and driving Kenya's economic growth. S...

item.title: Nigeria Police Recruitment: Physical Screening for 50,000 Constables Kicks Off in Port Harcourt
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force don start physical and credential screening for the recruitment of 50,000 Police Constables today for Port Harcourt. CSP Grace Iringe-Koko, the State Police Public Relations O...

item.title: Using the NHI Pause to Strengthen Health System Foundations
item.description: The recent court-ordered pause in implementing South Africa's National Health Insurance (NHI) Act offers an opportunity to address the structural weaknesses in the country's health system. Experts Dr ...

item.title: John Swinney condemns violence at Rangers-Celtic match and discusses football safety measures
item.description: Scotland's First Minister, John Swinney, has strongly condemned the disorder that occurred at last weekend's Old Firm match between Rangers and Celtic. During the match, fans reportedly stormed the pi...

item.title: Senate Deadlock Over DHS Funding Continues Amid Security Concerns
item.description: The ongoing U.S. government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has reached nearly a month, with Democrats and Republicans at an impasse. Senators Adam Schiff (D-Calif.) and M...

item.title: Human Rights Groups Recognise Reports on Tanzania Election Unrest Missed Some Views
item.description: On March 12, 2026, during a side event at the 61st UN Human Rights Council in Geneva, Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch acknowledged that their reports on the alleged human rights violation...

item.title: Two early-morning crashes on M1 corridor leave three men in critical condition
item.description: Early on Saturday morning, two separate crashes on the M1 corridor in Queensland left three men in critical condition. The first incident occurred around 1am at the Brigade Drive roundabout under the ...

item.title: How corruption in counties weakens Kenya’s healthcare system
item.description: This editorial argues that corruption in Kenya’s county governments is directly undermining the delivery of quality healthcare services to ordinary citizens. It explains that since devolution, countie...

item.title: Health Experts Criticise New MAIFIP Guidelines for Failing to Prevent Political Interference
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – A group of health development experts from the Universal Health Care (UHC) Collective has expressed concerns that the Medical Assistance for Indigent and Financially Incapacitate...

item.title: Oil Prices See Weekly Rise Amid Middle East Tensions and Limited US Measures
item.description: Oil prices continued to climb this week despite the United States issuing a 30-day waiver allowing countries to buy Russian oil and petroleum products stuck at sea. Brent crude for May went up by $1.0...

item.title: Pakistan urges action over ongoing closure of Jama Masjid in Srinagar during Ramadan
item.description: Pakistan has strongly condemned the ongoing closure of the historic Jama Masjid in Srinagar, located in Indian Illegally Occupied Jammu and Kashmir (IIOJK), on the last Friday of Ramzan, highlighting ...

item.title: Trump claims US targeted military installations on Iran's Kharg Island amid regional tensions
item.description: Former President Donald Trump asserted that the United States executed what he described as one of the most powerful bombing operations in Middle Eastern history, targeting military facilities on Iran...

item.title: Jessie Buckley Emerges as Strong Favorite to Win Best Actress Oscar for Hamnet
item.description: Irish actress Jessie Buckley is poised to make history as the potential first Irish winner of the Best Actress Oscar, with experts considering her victory nearly assured ahead of the 2026 Academy Awar...

item.title: Sailor Trapped in Gulf Shares Experiences Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: A sailor named Wang Shang, aged 32, from Henan province in China, is currently stranded in the Gulf on a foreign vessel transporting liquefied petroleum gas. The ship cannot pass through the Strait of...
